member.name: Jerome Mayhew
member.synopsis: Jerome Mayhew is the Conservative MP for Broadland and Fakenham , and has been an MP continually since 12 December 2019. He currently undertakes the roles of Shadow Minister (Transport), and Opposition Whip (Commons).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Heathrow Airport: South West Main Line
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, with reference to the Draft Heathrow Expansion National Policy Statement, of June 2026, what steps are being taken by her department and its agencies to support the Heathrow Southern Railway proposal to link So
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the importance of surface access to any future expansion of Heathrow Airport. The draft Heathrow Expansion National Policy Statement states that enhanced rail connectivity is necessary to support expansion and achieve public tran...

writtenQuestions.heading: Chris Gibb
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the answer of 15 June 2026 to Question 6815, on Chris Gibb, for what reason was the appointment of Mr Gibb terminated by Sir Andrew Haines.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Chris Gibb was dismissed as a non-executive director of DfT Operator Limited after breaching his contract. This led the Chair to lose trust and confidence in his ability to carry out the duties of a non-executive director.

writtenQuestions.heading: EGNOS
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the answer of 24 June 2026, to Question 10352, on EGNOS, whether the review is being undertaken in-house or by an external organisation; and whether HM Government would be required to pay the EU for
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport’s review of the transport case and the practicalities of rejoining the European Geostationary Navigation Overlay System (EGNOS) will be conducted by officials. Any decision to rejoin the EGNOS programme would be subject to agr...

writtenQuestions.heading: Parking Offences: Bournemouth, Christchurch and Poole
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the answer of 2 June 2026, to Question 3821, on Parking Offences: Bournemouth, Christchurch and Poole, if she will place in the Library a copy of the request from the Council to levy higher parking
writtenQuestions.answerText: I have placed a copy of Bournemouth, Christchurch and Poole Council’s request to trial higher-level Penalty Charge Notices in the Libraries of the House.

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads: Development Consent Orders
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how many road schemes with Development Consent Orders granted are under review by (a) her Department and (b) National Highways.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport has indicated that it will not be possible to answer this question within the usual time period. An answer is being prepared and will be provided as soon as it is available.

writtenQuestions.answerText: Specialist resource, such as airspace designers, for the delivery of Airspace Modernisation are provided by private companies such as NATS. NATS is a private company, and it is for its management to decide how to resource its operation and provide its ser...

writtenQuestions.heading: Hammersmith Bridge: Repairs and Maintenance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what recent discussions he has had with (a) Hammersmith and Fulham Council and (b) Transport for London since on funding options for the restoration of Hammersmith Bridge since 1 January 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: My Department engages regularly with London local highway authorities on matters related to their local highways networks, including both the London Borough of Hammersmith and Fulham and Transport for London. The technical advisory body to the Structures ...

writtenQuestions.heading: A46: Newark
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, for what reason National Highways cancelled its contract with Skanska for the A46 Newark Bypass scheme; and whether the scheme remains part of the strategic roads programme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The contract for the A46 scheme was awarded to Skanska in 2022. The Government inherited a series of unaffordable commitments in July 2024 and conducted a Spending Review. As a result, the A46 scheme was expected to be delivered later than previously assu...

writtenQuestions.answerText: The railway in Northern Ireland is a fully devolved matter, including assessment of any new rail projects and proposals. The Northern Ireland Executive is responsible for their Infrastructure Strategy, including cross border cooperation with the Republic ...

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department does not hold information on the purpose for which a minibus involved in a road collision was being used. The number of reported personal injury road casualties in collisions involving a minibus by casualty severity from 2015 to 2024 in Gre...

writtenQuestions.heading: Bus Services: Cycleways
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how many members of the Disabled Persons Transport Advisory Committee who contributed to its submissions on floating bus stops dated (a) 24 October 2025 and (b) 2 December 2025 were blind or partially sighted.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We cannot provide details of individual impairments. Under the Transport Act 1985, so far as reasonably practicable, at least 50 per cent of DPTAC’s membership should be made up of people who identify as disabled, making sure that lived experience informs...

writtenQuestions.heading: Aviation: Training
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, if she will publish a list of airport-based training colleges and academies in the UK, together with the tuition fees or course costs for each programme where this information is held.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport and the Civil Aviation Authority do not hold such a list at present but are exploring making a list of airport-based colleges public for the benefit of future students, working with DWP, Skills England, and DfE.

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport and the Civil Aviation Authority do not collect data on students completing training at airport-based training colleges and academies who enter employment in the aviation sector within 12 months of completing their course. Thi...

writtenQuestions.answerText: It is likely that vehicles which can recharge mostly or entirely at their own depot will reach a lower total cost of ownership than diesel first, whereas vehicles which rely more heavily on public charging, for example because they travel further from the...

writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to Question UIN 12840 answered on 30 June 2026.

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) Regulations 2026 make it against the law for anyone other than the learner to book their driving test. This is a civil law restriction, not a criminal one. Something that is "against the law" is ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Large Goods Vehicles: Electric Vehicles
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, in which year does her Department expect upfront purchase costs of zero-emission heavy goods vehicles to reach parity with equivalent diesel vehicles.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the answer of 24 June to UIN 11454.

Director of Walsingham Estate Company Ltd (farming, land and property management, retail and finance). I have not received any payment for this role. It will continue to be unpaid until further notice. Date interest arose: 25 June 2020 (Registered 6 July 2020; updated 17 November 2021)
